# Slide Spec Docs

> Canonical documentation for Slide Spec, a YAML-first static site and presentation system. Use these pages for the validated file formats, built-in slide templates, CLI workflow, and worked examples.

Important notes:
- Validation is strict. Prefer the schema reference over inference from examples.
- Read the four project files in order: `site.yaml` -> `presentations/index.yaml` -> `presentation.yaml` -> `generated.yaml`.
- `generated.yaml` may be hand-authored or connector-produced; GitHub is the only built-in fetch source today.
- Template pages explain which values come from authored content versus generated data.

## Getting Started

- [Slide Spec overview](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/): Product overview, core value proposition, and entry points into the docs.
- [Quickstart](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/quickstart): Recommended first path from install through init, validate, build, serve, and optional fetch.
- [Concepts](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/concepts): Core mental model for content structure, template-driven slides, generated data, and validation.

## Schema Reference

- [Schema overview](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/schema/): Explains how site.yaml, presentations/index.yaml, presentation.yaml, and generated.yaml fit together.
- [site.yaml schema](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/schema/site): Global branding, navigation labels, external links, and site-level configuration.
- [presentations/index.yaml schema](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/schema/presentations-index): Presentation catalog, publish state, featured flags, and file path references.
- [presentation.yaml schema](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/schema/presentation): Slide definitions, template ids, authored slide content, and presentation metadata.
- [generated.yaml schema](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/schema/generated): Generated or manually authored metrics, releases, contributors, and other computed content.

## Templates

- [Template index](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/): Summary of every built-in slide template and how template validation works.
- [Hero template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/hero): Opening slide for project identity, title framing, and top-level messaging.
- [Agenda template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/agenda): Auto-generated table of contents slide driven by the presentation structure.
- [Section list grid template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/section-list-grid): Grid layout for grouped lists, themes, or workstream summaries.
- [Timeline template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/timeline): Release or milestone cards sourced from generated data.
- [Progress timeline template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/progress-timeline): Self-contained staged progress slide authored directly in presentation content.
- [People template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/people): Contributor or team spotlight slide for profiles, roles, and recognition.
- [Metrics and links template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/metrics-and-links): Metric tiles plus linked references for status reporting and supporting resources.
- [Action cards template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/action-cards): Call-to-action card grid for follow-ups, asks, or next-step links.
- [Closing template](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/templates/closing): Final slide for wrap-up messaging and outbound links.

## CLI

- [CLI overview](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/): Command-line workflow and the relationship between init, validate, fetch, build, and serve.
- [init command](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/init): Scaffolds a new Slide Spec project with starter content.
- [fetch command](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/fetch): Populates generated.yaml from configured connectors, currently GitHub-backed.
- [build command](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/build): Produces the static site output.
- [serve command](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/serve): Builds and serves the site locally for preview.
- [validate command](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/cli/validate): Runs strict schema and template validation against the project content.

## Connectors

- [Connectors overview](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/connectors/): How connectors feed generated.yaml and where external data enters the project.
- [GitHub connector](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/connectors/github): Explains the GitHub-backed data source used by fetch and generated.yaml.

## Examples

- [Examples index](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/): Overview of the included example projects and how to choose among them.
- [Open source update example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/open-source-update): Quarterly community-update style project with releases, contributors, and roadmap data.
- [Product review example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/product-review): Product-management reporting example focused on milestones, initiatives, and customer themes.
- [Security posture example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/security-posture): Security-reporting example for vulnerabilities, patch status, and compliance metrics.
- [Community update example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/community-update): Community and DevRel example for engagement metrics, events, and highlights.
- [Tutorial example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/tutorial-example): Step-by-step walkthrough aligned with the reference project fixture.
- [Manual data example](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/examples/manual-data-example): Example project that authors generated.yaml by hand without a connector.

## Meta

- [Meta index](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/): Index of documentation policies, publishing notes, and support guidance.
- [Accessibility notes](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/accessibility): Accessibility goals, test coverage, and current expectations for the docs and app.
- [AI usage notes](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/ai): Guidance for tools consuming these docs, including stable entry points and common pitfalls.
- [SBOM notes](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/sbom): Release SBOM format, where to find published artifacts, and what repository contents they cover.
- [Agent assistance policy](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/agent-assistance): How maintainers expect coding agents to work with the repository and generated content.
- [Supply chain notes](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/supply-chain): Dependency, provenance, and release-process notes relevant to implementation work.
- [Cloudflare Pages deployment guide](https://docs.slide-spec.dev/meta/cloudflare-pages): Recommended deployment model for static Slide Spec sites built from committed source.
